title: Music Jingles - jingles_HIT03
author: Kenney Vleugels (Kenney.nl)
configRemovedNote: The SOUND_CONFIGS['zoneCaptured'] entry was removed in task/small-truth-fixes (campaign greenlight-followthrough). The zone_captured handler plays the positional zoneCapturedLocal variant pool (added in 0e42bc1a), so this single sting was never played and only cost a background fetch+decode on every client. The .ogg is DELIBERATELY retained on disk as rollback insurance (owner called the old sound 'acceptable for now'); re-adding a one-line SOUND_CONFIGS entry restores it.
attributionText: "Music Jingles" (jingles_HIT03) by Kenney Vleugels (kenney.nl), CC0 1.0 Universal (public domain) — credit appreciated but not required.
notes: Closes the AudioManager.ts zone_captured event-bus subscription that already called play('zoneCaptured', ...) with no matching SOUND_CONFIGS entry (silent no-op since the subscription was added). Picked the 'Hit jingles' sub-pack over the pack's 8-Bit/Pizzicato/Sax/Steel-drum jingles as the most tonally neutral (a short abstract percussive sting, not tied to a specific cheerful instrument timbre) for a gritty jungle-combat game; HIT03 chosen for being the most substantial of the very-short HIT variants (0.75s vs 0.28-0.6s) so a territorial capture reads as a touch more significant than a plain UI blip, while still short enough not to step on combat audio. Fetched directly from kenney.nl (HTTP 200, no auth/credentials needed) — same no-auth-direct-download approach as the existing incompetech.com precedent in this directory. Not waveform/listen-verified by the agent (no audio playback available in this environment); flagged for an owner spot-check, trivially swappable (one file + one SOUND_CONFIGS path) if the tone doesn't land.
